2606.00141.title: Adaptive data selection improves wearable prediction under low baseline performance
2606.00141.paper_summary: Adaptive sensing strategies that selectively sample data are increasingly used in wearable health systems to improve prediction performance under limited data budgets, yet their benefits across individuals remain poorly understood. Here, we evaluate adaptive selection of time windows for model training under fixed measurement budgets across multiple sensing modalities, including heart rate, activity, and ecological momentary assessment (EMA), in a longitudinal wearable dataset. We quantify performance gains relative to random sampling using both area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) and F1 score. Adaptive strategies yield substantial improvements in AUROC for participants with low baseline performance (with gains up to 0.7), while offering limited or negative gains for participants with strong baselines. Across modalities, adaptive gain is strongly inversely correlated with baseline performance (Pearson r = -0.67; Spearman p = -0.62). At the participant level, most individuals benefit in AUROC (60-80% across modalities), although improvements in F1 are smaller and less consistent. These findings show that adaptive sensing is not uniformly beneficial, but instead provides the greatest value in underperforming settings. Our results support selective deployment strategies that tailor adaptive sensing based on baseline performance to improve efficiency in wearable health monitoring.
2606.00141.paper_summary_zh: 適應性感測策略在可穿戴健康系統中越來越多地被用來在有限的數據預算下提高預測性能，但其在不同個體間的益處仍然不甚明瞭。<lb/>在此，我們評估在固定測量預算下，針對多種感測模式（包括心率、活動和生態瞬時評估（EMA））進行模型訓練的時間窗口的適應性選擇，基於一個長期的可穿戴數據集。<lb/>我們使用接收者操作特徵曲線下面積（AUROC）和F1分數量化相對於隨機抽樣的性能增益。<lb/>對於基線性能較低的參與者，適應性策略在AUROC上帶來了顯著的改善（增益高達0.7），而對於基線較強的參與者則提供有限或負的增益。<lb/>在各種模式中，適應性增益與基線性能呈強烈的負相關（Pearson r = -0.67; Spearman p = -0.62）。<lb/>在參與者層面，大多數個體在AUROC上受益（在各種模式中為60-80%），儘管F1的改善較小且不那麼一致。<lb/>這些發現顯示，適應性感測並非普遍有益，而是在表現不佳的環境中提供最大的價值。<lb/>我們的結果支持根據基線性能量身定制的適應性感測選擇性部署策略，以提高可穿戴健康監測的效率。
2606.00141.author: Ali Kargarandehkordi et.al.
